When Play Hurts: Understanding Common Barriers in Movement-Based Games

Sebastian Cmentowski, Sukran Karaosmanoglu, Frank Steinicke, Regina Bernhaupt

CHI ’26 (2026)

1FastPub
HOOK

Why This Matters

  • Exergames promise fun exercise but often drive players away through injuries, embarrassment, and addiction.
2FastPub
PROBLEM

The Problem

  • Despite widespread commercial success, exergames suffer from poor long-term retention; prior research examined isolated barriers in single titles rather than providing a cross-platform view of real user problems.
3FastPub
METHOD

Survey Methodology

  • Hybrid thematic analysis combining codebook and reflexive approaches
  • Screening ensured English fluency and active video game play
  • 60 distinct barriers, 44 effects, and 53 mitigation strategies identified
4FastPub
RESULTS

Barrier Categories and Platform Differences

  • Environmental and physical barriers most frequent overall
  • Mental barriers prominent for smartphone and arcade platforms
  • 21 barriers accounted for 77% of all reports
5FastPub
SIGNIFICANCE

Why It Matters

  • The systematic catalog of barriers and platform differences supplies concrete, actionable guidance for designing more adaptive, safe, and engaging movement-based games that can sustain physical activity.
6FastPub
TAKEAWAY

Key Takeaways

  • A large-scale online survey of 174 active and former players collected 621 barrier reports that were thematically analyzed across six categories, effects, and mitigation strategies.
  • 60 distinct barriers identified; 21 accounted for 77% of reports
  • The systematic catalog of barriers and platform differences supplies concrete, actionable guidance for designing more adaptive, safe, and engaging movement-based games that can sustain physical activity.
7FastPub
